pains.summary: Sales leadership doesn't know where to find marketing information or what actions marketing is taking
pains.quote: they don't know where to go. Or if we have this information on, you know, their leads, prospects, what actions they're taking.
pains.speaker: Kimberly Chen

pains.summary: Terrence (CRO) already looks at middle-to-bottom funnel metrics in Looker and Gong, so RevSure needs to fill the marketing gap
pains.quote: he is already looking at those kinds of numbers in our looker dashboards in gong right for forecasting so i think where we really want rupture to fill in the gap is more on the marketing side of things
pains.speaker: Kimberly Chen

pains.summary: Difficulty tracking event ROI - can't easily answer what they got out of events last year
pains.quote: sometimes it's not as easy to say like, well, this was the lead source for this opportunity, right? We already had this opportunity underway and it's still going strong and this was just a touch point.
pains.speaker: Emily Worth

pains.summary: Redwood customer not engaging with product - skill gap plus lack of effort to use the product
pains.quote: And we also haven't seen the effort for them to try and use the product.
pains.speaker: Ram Arunachalam

pains.summary: No good automated way to set segment when deals are put into Salesforce
pains.quote: As those deals are getting put into Salesforce and whatever, we don't really have a good automated way to set that segment yet
pains.speaker: Cory Perry

pains.summary: Data sync issues causing inaccurate campaign member email percentages
pains.quote: there was a sync that was happening that had not like fully completed by the time later For from Budwood right so that day they have made the change to the campaign member emails but that wasn't reflected by the time we presented to brian
pains.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

pains.summary: Discrepancy between RevSure and sales leader's closed won numbers - January looked inflated
pains.quote: it looks like the number in January was quite high, so it was around 400k, but it didn't happen and that maybe happened in December, but RevSure sees it as in January.
pains.speaker: Marco

tools_switched_from.reason: Already using for forecasting and middle-to-bottom funnel metrics, but missing marketing attribution and top-of-funnel visibility
tools_switched_from.quote: he is already looking at those kinds of numbers in our looker dashboards in gong right for forecasting so i think where we really want rupture to fill in the gap is more on the marketing side of things
tools_switched_from.speaker: Kimberly Chen

political_tussles.tension_label: Sales doesn't have visibility into marketing actions
political_tussles.summary: Sales team lacks access to RevSure and can't see what marketing is doing, creating friction
political_tussles.quote: i don't think they have access right to rupture Can find it difficult if we don't have like the information syncing over to Salesforce to understand like what marketing actions are being taken
political_tussles.speaker: Kimberly Chen

contrarians.claim: Adding a Stage Zero that is explicitly excluded from all pipeline metrics - a pre-pipeline holding stage
contrarians.why_unusual: Most RevOps teams fight to include MORE in pipeline, not create explicit non-pipeline stages. Stage Zero with 0% probability and omitted from forecast is counter to typical 'pipeline coverage' mentality.
contrarians.quote: we're essentially going to add a new stage, but that stage is going to be stage zero. And this will be a stage that will be at the beginning of the cycle omitted from any pipeline or anything like that. So it won't be included as It won't be included as part of any pipeline or anything.
contrarians.speaker: Cory Perry

contrarians.claim: Using custom 'year one ARR' field instead of standard Amount field because Amount includes one-time fees
contrarians.why_unusual: Most companies just use standard Amount field; deliberately excluding implementation/professional services revenue from pipeline metrics is a sophisticated (and unusual) approach to ARR purity.
contrarians.quote: it's a field called year one ARR. And so that's kind of our, that's kind of our like revenue field, I guess, if you want to say that's like our recurring revenue field is the year one ARR, whereas the amount field typically can be a combination of both whatever the recurring revenue is, and then also if there are any like one-time fees or implementation fees or anything like that they all get rolled up into that amount like that standard amount field
contrarians.speaker: Cory Perry

contrarians.claim: Terrence (CRO) cares more about product breakdown than source/channel breakdown of pipeline
contrarians.why_unusual: CROs typically obsess over 'where is pipeline coming from' (marketing vs sales); asking 'what products is it' first is unusual product-centric revenue leadership.
contrarians.quote: Terrence is typically asking about like cool. What products is that? Like what is the product breakdown or product makeup of that? versus you know, like Where it's coming from and you know things like that.
contrarians.speaker: Cory Perry

revsure_problems.problem: Data sync delay caused campaign member email percentage to show 20% when it should have been 10%
revsure_problems.quote: there was a sync that was happening that had not like fully completed by the time later For from Budwood right so that day they have made the change to the campaign member emails but that wasn't reflected by the time we presented to brian right because when i looked at it this morning like i didn't change like the filters or anything and it showed um 10 now instead of 20 for email marketing as a whole as a channel
revsure_problems.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

revsure_problems.problem: Close date discrepancy - RevSure showing January close when it may have been December
revsure_problems.quote: it looks like the number in January was quite high, so it was around 400k, but it didn't happen and that maybe happened in December, but RevSure sees it as in January.
revsure_problems.speaker: Marco

revsure_problems.problem: Confusion about whether 'interrupted' status means in progress or failed
revsure_problems.quote: depending it'll say interrupted but sometimes interrupted will mean it's in progress but that terminology is very Right, because even I like I'll look at it and I'll say interrupt him, but I say it's building but like that doesn't Clearly tell me if it is or if it you know
revsure_problems.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

best_about_revsure.summary: Cory thinks the dashboard is exactly what Terrence is looking for - quick value with segmentation
best_about_revsure.quote: this is pretty much, I think, on point to kind of what I think Terrence is looking for in terms of just quickly being able to come in, look for a particular value or whatever, but also being able to kind of segment this by different. by different things the way you're doing it here i think is definitely what he's looking for
best_about_revsure.speaker: Cory Perry

best_about_revsure.summary: Marco thinks the dashboard looks really good and can use for board reporting
best_about_revsure.quote: this is what i can use for uh our reporting to the board Great.
best_about_revsure.speaker: Marco

best_about_revsure.summary: Cory likes that they can see total touch points and drill into account activity
best_about_revsure.quote: 80 total touch points in the last 30 days if i'm looking at this right yep
best_about_revsure.speaker: Cory Perry

best_about_revsure.summary: Marco impressed by day-over-day cumulative pipeline comparison across quarters
best_about_revsure.quote: that's cool it's set to um new business
best_about_revsure.speaker: Marco

immediate.win: Can now see event campaign influence on pipeline - who attended, what opportunities touched
immediate.quote: here we're seeing the current fiscal quarter performance, right, for all contacts on the account, which the lot looking at the last touch filtered specifically to show you all the campaigns that have been logged right as a campaign type of event.
immediate.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

immediate.win: Can write last touch campaign back to Salesforce so sales reps know what was fresh in prospect's mind
immediate.quote: we can tell you oh to the sales rep right write it back to salesforce this was their last touch campaign right so they have that insight as to seeing like oh the last thing this person saw was this so that conversation for them that's what's fresh for them in their mind
immediate.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

immediate.win: Email marketing percentage corrected from 20% to 10% after sync completed
immediate.quote: when i looked at it this morning like i didn't change like the filters or anything and it showed um 10 now instead of 20 for email marketing as a whole as a channel
immediate.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

long_term.win: Full funnel insights connecting marketing to sales to customer success for holistic account planning
long_term.quote: we're trying to like shift gears i think going into q2 into more like holistic account planning on both like the marketing sales and cs side of things
long_term.speaker: Kimberly Chen

long_term.win: Re-engage conversations about additional RevSure tools (data enrichment, intent) now that they're evaluating new data vendor
long_term.quote: we kind of have a list of different data vendors and stuff that we're sort of, I think, evaluating through at this point. But I say that to say, I think this could potentially maybe open the conversation back up around some of these other tools that I know you guys are coming, you know, kind of coming to the table with.
long_term.speaker: Cory Perry

long_term.win: Sales team adoption of RevSure through Salesforce sync and better visibility
long_term.quote: we can export the data in Rapture so that it can be visualized in the manner that is most actionable for you know for our customers.
long_term.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

before_state.pain_summary: No proper marketing attribution; UTM tracking broken since 2021; manual parsing of URLs to extract campaign data; no way to map inbounds until August; duplicated HubSpot forms; sales team not logging leads properly; only new business opportunities tracked, excluding renewals/cross-sell

after_state.with_revsure: Building toward unified attribution with campaign channel classification, white space account dashboards with marketing touch correlation, event influence tracking, and cohort-based funnel views
wins_realized: Event dashboard with last-touch and influence attribution
Ability to filter by customer health score and sentiment
Terrence dashboard created with 6 core views
Event campaign attribution showing prospect/customer/lost client breakdown
Lead prioritization by account tier and activity recency
Last touch campaign write-back to Salesforce enabled
open_issues: Campaign channel classification still in progress
Custom fields require manual backend ingestion
Sales team adoption for lead logging
Need to add product count/penetration fields
Campaign member status not yet synced (registered vs attended)
Custom year one ARR field prevents tracking amount changes
Redwood account still has adoption challenges
Some data sync delays causing temporary metric inaccuracies
